rulings.Case Title: GEORGE TYLER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, January 13, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m. Defendant Wismer and Becker¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Workers¿ compensation is not plaintiff¿s exclusive remedy because plaintiff alleges asbestos exposure by the activities of defendant¿s employees while plaintiff was employed by a company other than defendant. The moving party failed to sustain its burden of production. Defendant relies solely on plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and fails to cite to any ¿all facts¿ interrogatory responses regarding asbestos exposure. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT KEATING VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, January 13, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m. Defendant Square D¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its initial burden of production and plaintiff failed to raise a triable issue whether the products in question contained asbestos. Plaintiff¿s discovery responses and deposition testimony were factually devoid of any information showing that the Square D products plaintiff worked with or around contained asbestos. Kenneth Cohen¿s expert declaration and defendant¿s admission as to ¿some¿ products containing asbestos did not create a triable issue of fact as to whether the products contained asbestos. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MILTON LIPMAN et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, January 13, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m. Defendant Federal Pacific Electric¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. The affidavit of Robert Steel shifted the burden to plaintiffs to demonstrate a triable issue whether any of defendant¿s products contained asbestos. Plaintiffs¿ objections to Steel¿s affidavit are overruled. Plaintiffs¿ evidence fails to demonstrate a triable issue because it only bears on the issue of exposure to Federal Pacific Electric¿s products, not whether those products contained asbestos.= (220/HEK)
